name: Yokohama (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity and the city's openness as Japan's principal port

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised red emblem composed of the kanji æ¿± (hama, 'beach') drawn in a flowing form.
history: The Yokohama City emblem was officially adopted on 5 June 1909, and the flag bearing the red emblem on a white field has been used as the city flag since the same year. The emblem stylises the character æ¿± (hama, 'beach') from æ¨ªæ¿± (Yokohama) in a way that evokes ocean waves, recalling the city's identity as Japan's first major treaty port opened to foreign trade in 1859.

features.symbolism: a calligraphic abstraction of the character æ¿± ('beach', the second kanji in æ¨ªæ¿±/Yokohama) drawn to evoke flowing waves; the design dates to 1909 and recalls Yokohama's role as Japan's first international port
